value_chain_position: Super-app payment and marketplace rails for Central Asia — operates Kazakhstan's dominant fintech ecosystem combining payments, e-commerce marketplace, and digital banking on a single app; the toll-taker on ~80% of Kazakhstan's digital payment volume.
relative_comps: KSPI is arguably the most load-bearing fintech in its geography — it has no meaningful domestic competitor and processes the majority of Kazakhstan's digital payments. Its 51% YoY revenue growth and 46% 3-year CAGR are among the best in the fintech universe. The comparison universe must be EM super-apps (MELI, Grab, Sea) rather than US fintechs — KSPI's TAM is Kazakhstan + Central Asia expansion. The primary risks are FX, sovereign risk, and sanctions exposure given Kazakhstan's Russia proximity. At ~$15B mcap vs $216B TTM revenue (likely in KZT, not USD — the raw number confirms local-currency reporting), the real USD financials make this a $4-5B revenue business at current FX, still remarkable for the cap.
